haiden.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}{{ill|lt=Tatsuno City|Q877190|WD=Q877190}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ibo Town|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Ibo Town Nakatomi|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakatomi}} |Coordinates = {{WikiCoordinates2|34|50|27.3|N|134|32|27.4|E|scale:25000:region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
Also enshrines {{ill|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Ōkashima-no-Kami}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkayama-toomi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, the deities of the Engishiki-listed Awaji Shrine |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myōjin Taisha}})
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinsen-heihaku-ryō kyōshin-sha|1=UNKNOWN}} |Founded = Traditionally {{ill|lt=Hōki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676}} 1 ({{ill|lt=770|Q25021|WD=Q25021}}) |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |Alternative name = |Pilgrimage site = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} |Rituals = |Map= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Nakatomi
Itate Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Nakatomi Itate Shrine''' (なかとみ いたてじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}{{ill|lt=Tatsuno City|Q877190|WD=Q877190}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ibo Town|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Ibo Town Nakatomi|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakatomi}}. It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}}, classified as a {{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}, a former prefectural shrine, and a former shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and heihaku. Because it is located in the district named Nakatomi (pronounced Nakajin),The former {{ill|lt=Nishiharima|Q11628288|WD=Q11628288}} region’s {{ill|lt=Isei District|Q11498436|WD=Q11498436}} Nakatomi Village ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakajin Village|1=UNKNOWN}}), Nakatomi of {{ill|lt=Ibo Village|Q17193534|WD=Q17193534}}, later part of {{ill|lt=Tatsuno City|Q2570410|WD=Q2570410}} it is locally called “Nakajin–Indatsu Shrine,” using the on’yomi reading. It enshrines together the Engishiki-listed '''Awaji Shrine''' through {{ill|lt=enshrinement together|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} At present, it also enshrines {{ill|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Ōkashima-no-Kami}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkayama-toomi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, the deities of the Engishiki Awaji Shrine. === History === It is traditionally said to have been founded on the 15th day of the 6th month of {{ill|lt=Hōki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676}} 1, and it is listed as a {{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}. From the medieval period, however, due to {{ill|lt=Ryōbu Shintō|Q4402813|WD=Q4402813}}, ascetic practitioners became involved in shrine affairs and the Twelve Gongen that had been on the mountain behind the shrine were enshrined together in the main shrine. Until the early modern period, the shrine was called {{ill|lt=Zaō Gongen|Q10514059|WD=Q10514059}}. On October 10 of Meiji 10, after a formal petition, it reverted to its original name, Nakatomi Itate Shrine. In the same month and year,Also said to be May of Meiji 12 it was ranked as a prefectural shrine. === Chronology === : {{ill|lt=Hōki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676}} 1 ({{ill|lt=770|Q25021|WD=Q25021}}) June 15 – Foundation : {{ill|lt=Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021}}) – Five households granted as shrine estate to the Nakatomi deity : {{ill|lt=Shōtoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1713|Q6873|WD=Q6873}}) March – Construction : {{ill|lt=Man’en|Q1193862|WD=Q1193862}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1860|Q7691|WD=Q7691}}) – Construction : {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1877|Q7773|WD=Q7773}}) October 10 – Name restored to '''Nakatomi Itate Shrine''' : Meiji 12 ({{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783}}) May 15 – Ranked as a prefectural shrine : {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1916|Q2087|WD=Q2087}}) April 11 – Designated as a shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and heihaku === Shrine precincts === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki Tsubuza Amaterasu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Itsukushima Shrine|Q191763|WD=Q191763}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenman Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kitané Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yakushi Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Benzaiten|1=UNKNOWN}} === The Tsubuoka legend and the Engishiki Tsubuza Amaterasu Shrine === The {{ill|lt=Harima no Kuni Fudoki|Q11498726|WD=Q11498726}} records that the name “Tsubuoka,” the origin of the name Ibo Village in {{ill|lt=Ibo District|Q1042150|WD=Q1042150}}, comes from the story that “when {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ashihara Shikō-no-Mikoto}} came to this mountain to dispute possession of the land with {{ill|lt=Amenohiboko|Q713100|WD=Q713100|lt=Ame-no-Hiboko-no-Mikoto}} and ate a meal, grains of rice fell.”Lines 283–288 This Tsubuoka is identified with Mount Nakatomi behind Nakatomi Itate Shrine, and the Engishiki Tsubuza Amaterasu Shrine stands within the precincts.Sources such as “Jingishi-ryō,” “Harima Kagami,” “Harima Meisho Junran Zue,” and “Ibo District Gazetteer” also suggest a connection between the Engishiki Myōjin Taisha Tsubuza Amaterasu Shrine and the Tsubuoka tradition at the Hachiman Shrine located in Iseyano, Hayashida Village, Ibo District (present-day Kamiiise, Hayashida-chō, Himeji City). === Nakatomi Itate Shrine as a candidate for an Engishiki shrine === There are several theories: that this shrine located in Nakajin Village of {{ill|lt=Itō District|Q11498435|WD=Q11498435}} (Ibo Town Nakatomi, Tatsuno City) is the Engishiki Nakatomi Itate Shrine; that the Hachiman Shrine on Mount Matsuo in Okusami Village, Itō District (Hachiman, {{ill|lt=Hayashida Town|Q11533214|WD=Q11533214}}, {{ill|lt=Himeji City|Q209801|WD=Q209801}}) is the Engishiki Nakatomi Itate Shrine; and that the {{ill|lt=Uobuki Hachiman Shrine|Q11673954|WD=Q11673954}} located in Miyauchi Village, Itō District (Miyauchi, {{ill|lt=Aboshi Ward|Q11607184|WD=Q11607184}}, Himeji City) is the Engishiki Nakatomi Itate Shrine. === Engishiki Awaji Shrine === {{Shrine |Name = Awaji Shrine |Image = |Location = Enshrined together at Nakatomi Itate Shrine
Former location unknown |Coordinates = |Enshrined deities = {{ill|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Ōkashima-no-Kami}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkayama-toomi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} |Shrine rank = Traditionally Engishiki shrine |Founded = |Main hall = |Alternative name = |Pilgrimage site = |Annual festival = |Rituals = }} '''Awaji Shrine''' (あわちじんじゃ) is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki shrine|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}}. It is enshrined together at Nakatomi Itate Shrine in Ibo Town, Tatsuno City, Hyōgo Prefecture. During the Edo period, it was called Awaniwa Shrine. It is believed to have been enshrined together in the main hall of Nakatomi Itate Shrine sometime from the medieval period through the mid-Edo period, but the “Jingishi-ryō” identifies it with Yobira Shrine. === Access === * JR {{ill|lt=Kishin Line|Q869221|WD=Q869221}} {{ill|lt=Hon-Tatsuno Station|Q11520740|WD=Q11520740}}, about 10 minutes by taxi * JR {{ill|lt=San’yō Main Line|Q1193436|WD=Q1193436}}{{ill|lt=Tatsuno Station|Q5366259|WD=Q5366259}}, about 10 minutes by taxi * About 5 minutes north from the “Nishikama Intersection,” the terminus of {{ill|lt=National Route 2|Q1131340|WD=Q1131340}}{{ill|lt=Taishi–Tatsuno Bypass|Q11443881|WD=Q11443881}} === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Kumiai Chemical Industry|Q11298808|WD=Q11298808}} Tatsuno Plant * {{ill|lt=Hiraki (company)|Q11330294|WD=Q11330294|lt=Hiraki Co., Ltd.}} Tatsuno Store === Notes === === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Tsubuza Amaterasu Shrine|Q11604472|WD=Q11604472}} * {{ill|lt=Uobuki Hachiman Shrine|Q11673954|WD=Q11673954}} === External links === * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6318008.html Nakatomi Itate Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/dbindex/jinja/446001.html Nakatomi Itate Shrine (Ibo District, Harima Province)] (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program, “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection”) {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nakatomi Itate Shrine}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Hyōgo Prefecture}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nakatomi Itate Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nakatomi Itate Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:播磨国の式内社|名なかとみいたてしんしや]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧県社]] [[Category:揖保郡|社なかとみいたてしんしや]] [[Category:たつの市の建築物]] [[Category:たつの市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]]

Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}{{ill|lt=Tatsuno City|Q877190|WD=Q877190}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ibo Town|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Ibo Town Nakatomi|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakatomi}} |Coordinates = {{WikiCoordinates2|34|50|27.3|N|134|32|27.4|E|scale:25000:region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}

Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinsen-heihaku-ryō kyōshin-sha|1=UNKNOWN}} |Founded = Traditionally {{ill|lt=Hōki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676}} 1 ({{ill|lt=770|Q25021|WD=Q25021}}) |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |Alternative name = |Pilgrimage site = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} |Rituals = |Map= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Nakatomi
Itate Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Nakatomi Itate Shrine''' (なかとみ いたてじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}{{ill|lt=Tatsuno City|Q877190|WD=Q877190}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ibo Town|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Ibo Town Nakatomi|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakatomi}}. It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}}, classified as a {{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}, a former prefectural shrine, and a former shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and heihaku. Because it is located in the district named Nakatomi (pronounced Nakajin),The former {{ill|lt=Nishiharima|Q11628288|WD=Q11628288}} region’s {{ill|lt=Isei District|Q11498436|WD=Q11498436}} Nakatomi Village ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakajin Village|1=UNKNOWN}}), Nakatomi of {{ill|lt=Ibo Village|Q17193534|WD=Q17193534}}, later part of {{ill|lt=Tatsuno City|Q2570410|WD=Q2570410}} it is locally called “Nakajin–Indatsu Shrine,” using the on’yomi reading. It enshrines together the Engishiki-listed '''Awaji Shrine''' through {{ill|lt=enshrinement together|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} At present, it also enshrines {{ill|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Ōkashima-no-Kami}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkayama-toomi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, the deities of the Engishiki Awaji Shrine. === History === It is traditionally said to have been founded on the 15th day of the 6th month of {{ill|lt=Hōki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676}} 1, and it is listed as a {{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}. From the medieval period, however, due to {{ill|lt=Ryōbu Shintō|Q4402813|WD=Q4402813}}, ascetic practitioners became involved in shrine affairs and the Twelve Gongen that had been on the mountain behind the shrine were enshrined together in the main shrine. Until the early modern period, the shrine was called {{ill|lt=Zaō Gongen|Q10514059|WD=Q10514059}}. On October 10 of Meiji 10, after a formal petition, it reverted to its original name, Nakatomi Itate Shrine. In the same month and year,Also said to be May of Meiji 12 it was ranked as a prefectural shrine. === Chronology === : {{ill|lt=Hōki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676}} 1 ({{ill|lt=770|Q25021|WD=Q25021}}) June 15 – Foundation : {{ill|lt=Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021}}) – Five households granted as shrine estate to the Nakatomi deity : {{ill|lt=Shōtoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1713|Q6873|WD=Q6873}}) March – Construction : {{ill|lt=Man’en|Q1193862|WD=Q1193862}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1860|Q7691|WD=Q7691}}) – Construction : {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1877|Q7773|WD=Q7773}}) October 10 – Name restored to '''Nakatomi Itate Shrine''' : Meiji 12 ({{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783}}) May 15 – Ranked as a prefectural shrine : {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1916|Q2087|WD=Q2087}}) April 11 – Designated as a shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and heihaku === Shrine precincts === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki Tsubuza Amaterasu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Itsukushima Shrine|Q191763|WD=Q191763}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenman Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kitané Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yakushi Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Benzaiten|1=UNKNOWN}} === The Tsubuoka legend and the Engishiki Tsubuza Amaterasu Shrine === The {{ill|lt=Harima no Kuni Fudoki|Q11498726|WD=Q11498726}} records that the name “Tsubuoka,” the origin of the name Ibo Village in {{ill|lt=Ibo District|Q1042150|WD=Q1042150}}, comes from the story that “when {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ashihara Shikō-no-Mikoto}} came to this mountain to dispute possession of the land with {{ill|lt=Amenohiboko|Q713100|WD=Q713100|lt=Ame-no-Hiboko-no-Mikoto}} and ate a meal, grains of rice fell.”Lines 283–288 This Tsubuoka is identified with Mount Nakatomi behind Nakatomi Itate Shrine, and the Engishiki Tsubuza Amaterasu Shrine stands within the precincts.Sources such as “Jingishi-ryō,” “Harima Kagami,” “Harima Meisho Junran Zue,” and “Ibo District Gazetteer” also suggest a connection between the Engishiki Myōjin Taisha Tsubuza Amaterasu Shrine and the Tsubuoka tradition at the Hachiman Shrine located in Iseyano, Hayashida Village, Ibo District (present-day Kamiiise, Hayashida-chō, Himeji City). === Nakatomi Itate Shrine as a candidate for an Engishiki shrine === There are several theories: that this shrine located in Nakajin Village of {{ill|lt=Itō District|Q11498435|WD=Q11498435}} (Ibo Town Nakatomi, Tatsuno City) is the Engishiki Nakatomi Itate Shrine; that the Hachiman Shrine on Mount Matsuo in Okusami Village, Itō District (Hachiman, {{ill|lt=Hayashida Town|Q11533214|WD=Q11533214}}, {{ill|lt=Himeji City|Q209801|WD=Q209801}}) is the Engishiki Nakatomi Itate Shrine; and that the {{ill|lt=Uobuki Hachiman Shrine|Q11673954|WD=Q11673954}} located in Miyauchi Village, Itō District (Miyauchi, {{ill|lt=Aboshi Ward|Q11607184|WD=Q11607184}}, Himeji City) is the Engishiki Nakatomi Itate Shrine. === Engishiki Awaji Shrine === {{Shrine |Name = Awaji Shrine |Image = |Location = Enshrined together at Nakatomi Itate Shrine
Former location unknown |Coordinates = |Enshrined deities = {{ill|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Ōkashima-no-Kami}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkayama-toomi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} |Shrine rank = Traditionally Engishiki shrine |Founded = |Main hall = |Alternative name = |Pilgrimage site = |Annual festival = |Rituals = }} '''Awaji Shrine''' (あわちじんじゃ) is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki shrine|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}}. It is enshrined together at Nakatomi Itate Shrine in Ibo Town, Tatsuno City, Hyōgo Prefecture. During the Edo period, it was called Awaniwa Shrine. It is believed to have been enshrined together in the main hall of Nakatomi Itate Shrine sometime from the medieval period through the mid-Edo period, but the “Jingishi-ryō” identifies it with Yobira Shrine. === Access === * JR {{ill|lt=Kishin Line|Q869221|WD=Q869221}} {{ill|lt=Hon-Tatsuno Station|Q11520740|WD=Q11520740}}, about 10 minutes by taxi * JR {{ill|lt=San’yō Main Line|Q1193436|WD=Q1193436}}{{ill|lt=Tatsuno Station|Q5366259|WD=Q5366259}}, about 10 minutes by taxi * About 5 minutes north from the “Nishikama Intersection,” the terminus of {{ill|lt=National Route 2|Q1131340|WD=Q1131340}}{{ill|lt=Taishi–Tatsuno Bypass|Q11443881|WD=Q11443881}} === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Kumiai Chemical Industry|Q11298808|WD=Q11298808}} Tatsuno Plant * {{ill|lt=Hiraki (company)|Q11330294|WD=Q11330294|lt=Hiraki Co., Ltd.}} Tatsuno Store === Notes === === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Tsubuza Amaterasu Shrine|Q11604472|WD=Q11604472}} * {{ill|lt=Uobuki Hachiman Shrine|Q11673954|WD=Q11673954}} === External links === * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6318008.html Nakatomi Itate Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/dbindex/jinja/446001.html Nakatomi Itate Shrine (Ibo District, Harima Province)] (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program, “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection”) {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nakatomi Itate Shrine}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Hyōgo Prefecture}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nakatomi Itate Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nakatomi Itate Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:播磨国の式内社|名なかとみいたてしんしや]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧県社]] [[Category:揖保郡|社なかとみいたてしんしや]] [[Category:たつの市の建築物]] [[Category:たつの市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]]

haiden.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}{{ill|lt=Tatsuno City|Q877190|WD=Q877190}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ibo Town|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Ibo Town Nakatomi|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakatomi}} |Coordinates = {{WikiCoordinates2|34|50|27.3|N|134|32|27.4|E|scale:25000:region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
Also enshrines {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Ōkashima-no-Kami}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkayama-toomi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, the deities of the Engishiki-listed Awaji Shrine |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myōjin Taisha}})
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinsen-heihaku-ryō kyōshin-sha|1=UNKNOWN}} |Founded = Traditionally {{ill|lt=Hōki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676}} 1 ({{ill|lt=770|Q25021|WD=Q25021}}) |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |Alternative name = |Pilgrimage site = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} |Rituals = |Map= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Nakatomi
Itate Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Nakatomi Itate Shrine''' (なかとみ いたてじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}{{ill|lt=Tatsuno City|Q877190|WD=Q877190}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ibo Town|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Ibo Town Nakatomi|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakatomi}}. It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}}, classified as a {{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}, a former prefectural shrine, and a former shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and heihaku. Because it is located in the district named Nakatomi (pronounced Nakajin),The former {{ill|lt=Nishiharima|Q11628288|WD=Q11628288}} region’s {{ill|lt=Isei District|Q11498436|WD=Q11498436}} Nakatomi Village ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakajin Village|1=UNKNOWN}}), Nakatomi of {{ill|lt=Ibo Village|Q17193534|WD=Q17193534}}, later part of {{ill|lt=Tatsuno City|Q2570410|WD=Q2570410}} it is locally called “Nakajin–Indatsu Shrine,” using the on’yomi reading. It enshrines together the Engishiki-listed '''Awaji Shrine''' through {{ill|lt=enshrinement together|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} At present, it also enshrines {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Ōkashima-no-Kami}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkayama-toomi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, the deities of the Engishiki Awaji Shrine. === History === It is traditionally said to have been founded on the 15th day of the 6th month of {{ill|lt=Hōki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676}} 1, and it is listed as a {{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}. From the medieval period, however, due to {{ill|lt=Ryōbu Shintō|Q4402813|WD=Q4402813}}, ascetic practitioners became involved in shrine affairs and the Twelve Gongen that had been on the mountain behind the shrine were enshrined together in the main shrine. Until the early modern period, the shrine was called {{ill|lt=Zaō Gongen|Q10514059|WD=Q10514059}}. On October 10 of Meiji 10, after a formal petition, it reverted to its original name, Nakatomi Itate Shrine. In the same month and year,Also said to be May of Meiji 12 it was ranked as a prefectural shrine. === Chronology === : {{ill|lt=Hōki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676}} 1 ({{ill|lt=770|Q25021|WD=Q25021}}) June 15 – Foundation : {{ill|lt=Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021}}) – Five households granted as shrine estate to the Nakatomi deity : {{ill|lt=Shōtoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1713|Q6873|WD=Q6873}}) March – Construction : {{ill|lt=Man’en|Q1193862|WD=Q1193862}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1860|Q7691|WD=Q7691}}) – Construction : {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1877|Q7773|WD=Q7773}}) October 10 – Name restored to '''Nakatomi Itate Shrine''' : Meiji 12 ({{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783}}) May 15 – Ranked as a prefectural shrine : {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1916|Q2087|WD=Q2087}}) April 11 – Designated as a shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and heihaku === Shrine precincts === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki Tsubuza Amaterasu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Itsukushima Shrine|Q191763|WD=Q191763}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenman Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kitané Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yakushi Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Benzaiten|1=UNKNOWN}} === The Tsubuoka legend and the Engishiki Tsubuza Amaterasu Shrine === The {{ill|lt=Harima no Kuni Fudoki|Q11498726|WD=Q11498726}} records that the name “Tsubuoka,” the origin of the name Ibo Village in {{ill|lt=Ibo District|Q1042150|WD=Q1042150}}, comes from the story that “when {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ashihara Shikō-no-Mikoto}} came to this mountain to dispute possession of the land with {{ill|lt=Amenohiboko|Q713100|WD=Q713100|lt=Ame-no-Hiboko-no-Mikoto}} and ate a meal, grains of rice fell.”Lines 283–288 This Tsubuoka is identified with Mount Nakatomi behind Nakatomi Itate Shrine, and the Engishiki Tsubuza Amaterasu Shrine stands within the precincts.Sources such as “Jingishi-ryō,” “Harima Kagami,” “Harima Meisho Junran Zue,” and “Ibo District Gazetteer” also suggest a connection between the Engishiki Myōjin Taisha Tsubuza Amaterasu Shrine and the Tsubuoka tradition at the Hachiman Shrine located in Iseyano, Hayashida Village, Ibo District (present-day Kamiiise, Hayashida-chō, Himeji City). === Nakatomi Itate Shrine as a candidate for an Engishiki shrine === There are several theories: that this shrine located in Nakajin Village of {{ill|lt=Itō District|Q11498435|WD=Q11498435}} (Ibo Town Nakatomi, Tatsuno City) is the Engishiki Nakatomi Itate Shrine; that the Hachiman Shrine on Mount Matsuo in Okusami Village, Itō District (Hachiman, {{ill|lt=Hayashida Town|Q11533214|WD=Q11533214}}, {{ill|lt=Himeji City|Q209801|WD=Q209801}}) is the Engishiki Nakatomi Itate Shrine; and that the {{ill|lt=Uobuki Hachiman Shrine|Q11673954|WD=Q11673954}} located in Miyauchi Village, Itō District (Miyauchi, {{ill|lt=Aboshi Ward|Q11607184|WD=Q11607184}}, Himeji City) is the Engishiki Nakatomi Itate Shrine. === Engishiki Awaji Shrine === {{Shrine |Name = Awaji Shrine |Image = |Location = Enshrined together at Nakatomi Itate Shrine
Former location unknown |Coordinates = |Enshrined deities = {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Ōkashima-no-Kami}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkayama-toomi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} |Shrine rank = Traditionally Engishiki shrine |Founded = |Main hall = |Alternative name = |Pilgrimage site = |Annual festival = |Rituals = }} '''Awaji Shrine''' (あわちじんじゃ) is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki shrine|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}}. It is enshrined together at Nakatomi Itate Shrine in Ibo Town, Tatsuno City, Hyōgo Prefecture. During the Edo period, it was called Awaniwa Shrine. It is believed to have been enshrined together in the main hall of Nakatomi Itate Shrine sometime from the medieval period through the mid-Edo period, but the “Jingishi-ryō” identifies it with Yobira Shrine. === Access === * JR {{ill|lt=Kishin Line|Q869221|WD=Q869221}} {{ill|lt=Hon-Tatsuno Station|Q11520740|WD=Q11520740}}, about 10 minutes by taxi * JR {{ill|lt=San’yō Main Line|Q1193436|WD=Q1193436}}{{ill|lt=Tatsuno Station|Q5366259|WD=Q5366259}}, about 10 minutes by taxi * About 5 minutes north from the “Nishikama Intersection,” the terminus of {{ill|lt=National Route 2|Q1131340|WD=Q1131340}}{{ill|lt=Taishi–Tatsuno Bypass|Q11443881|WD=Q11443881}} === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Kumiai Chemical Industry|Q11298808|WD=Q11298808}} Tatsuno Plant * {{ill|lt=Hiraki (company)|Q11330294|WD=Q11330294|lt=Hiraki Co., Ltd.}} Tatsuno Store === Notes === === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Tsubuza Amaterasu Shrine|Q11604472|WD=Q11604472}} * {{ill|lt=Uobuki Hachiman Shrine|Q11673954|WD=Q11673954}} === External links === * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6318008.html Nakatomi Itate Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/dbindex/jinja/446001.html Nakatomi Itate Shrine (Ibo District, Harima Province)] (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program, “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection”) {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nakatomi Itate Shrine}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Hyōgo Prefecture}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nakatomi Itate Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nakatomi Itate Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:播磨国の式内社|名なかとみいたてしんしや]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧県社]] [[Category:揖保郡|社なかとみいたてしんしや]] [[Category:たつの市の建築物]] [[Category:たつの市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]]

Also enshrines {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Ōkashima-no-Kami}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkayama-toomi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, the deities of the Engishiki-listed Awaji Shrine |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myōjin Taisha}})
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinsen-heihaku-ryō kyōshin-sha|1=UNKNOWN}} |Founded = Traditionally {{ill|lt=Hōki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676}} 1 ({{ill|lt=770|Q25021|WD=Q25021}}) |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |Alternative name = |Pilgrimage site = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} |Rituals = |Map= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Nakatomi
Itate Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Nakatomi Itate Shrine''' (なかとみ いたてじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}{{ill|lt=Tatsuno City|Q877190|WD=Q877190}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ibo Town|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Ibo Town Nakatomi|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakatomi}}. It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}}, classified as a {{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}, a former prefectural shrine, and a former shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and heihaku. Because it is located in the district named Nakatomi (pronounced Nakajin),The former {{ill|lt=Nishiharima|Q11628288|WD=Q11628288}} region’s {{ill|lt=Isei District|Q11498436|WD=Q11498436}} Nakatomi Village ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakajin Village|1=UNKNOWN}}), Nakatomi of {{ill|lt=Ibo Village|Q17193534|WD=Q17193534}}, later part of {{ill|lt=Tatsuno City|Q2570410|WD=Q2570410}} it is locally called “Nakajin–Indatsu Shrine,” using the on’yomi reading. It enshrines together the Engishiki-listed '''Awaji Shrine''' through {{ill|lt=enshrinement together|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} At present, it also enshrines {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Ōkashima-no-Kami}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkayama-toomi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, the deities of the Engishiki Awaji Shrine. === History === It is traditionally said to have been founded on the 15th day of the 6th month of {{ill|lt=Hōki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676}} 1, and it is listed as a {{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}. From the medieval period, however, due to {{ill|lt=Ryōbu Shintō|Q4402813|WD=Q4402813}}, ascetic practitioners became involved in shrine affairs and the Twelve Gongen that had been on the mountain behind the shrine were enshrined together in the main shrine. Until the early modern period, the shrine was called {{ill|lt=Zaō Gongen|Q10514059|WD=Q10514059}}. On October 10 of Meiji 10, after a formal petition, it reverted to its original name, Nakatomi Itate Shrine. In the same month and year,Also said to be May of Meiji 12 it was ranked as a prefectural shrine. === Chronology === : {{ill|lt=Hōki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676}} 1 ({{ill|lt=770|Q25021|WD=Q25021}}) June 15 – Foundation : {{ill|lt=Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021}}) – Five households granted as shrine estate to the Nakatomi deity : {{ill|lt=Shōtoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1713|Q6873|WD=Q6873}}) March – Construction : {{ill|lt=Man’en|Q1193862|WD=Q1193862}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1860|Q7691|WD=Q7691}}) – Construction : {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1877|Q7773|WD=Q7773}}) October 10 – Name restored to '''Nakatomi Itate Shrine''' : Meiji 12 ({{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783}}) May 15 – Ranked as a prefectural shrine : {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1916|Q2087|WD=Q2087}}) April 11 – Designated as a shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and heihaku === Shrine precincts === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki Tsubuza Amaterasu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Itsukushima Shrine|Q191763|WD=Q191763}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenman Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kitané Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yakushi Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q818468|lt=Benzaiten|1=UNKNOWN}} === The Tsubuoka legend and the Engishiki Tsubuza Amaterasu Shrine === The {{ill|lt=Harima no Kuni Fudoki|Q11498726|WD=Q11498726}} records that the name “Tsubuoka,” the origin of the name Ibo Village in {{ill|lt=Ibo District|Q1042150|WD=Q1042150}}, comes from the story that “when {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ashihara Shikō-no-Mikoto}} came to this mountain to dispute possession of the land with {{ill|lt=Amenohiboko|Q713100|WD=Q713100|lt=Ame-no-Hiboko-no-Mikoto}} and ate a meal, grains of rice fell.”Lines 283–288 This Tsubuoka is identified with Mount Nakatomi behind Nakatomi Itate Shrine, and the Engishiki Tsubuza Amaterasu Shrine stands within the precincts.Sources such as “Jingishi-ryō,” “Harima Kagami,” “Harima Meisho Junran Zue,” and “Ibo District Gazetteer” also suggest a connection between the Engishiki Myōjin Taisha Tsubuza Amaterasu Shrine and the Tsubuoka tradition at the Hachiman Shrine located in Iseyano, Hayashida Village, Ibo District (present-day Kamiiise, Hayashida-chō, Himeji City). === Nakatomi Itate Shrine as a candidate for an Engishiki shrine === There are several theories: that this shrine located in Nakajin Village of {{ill|lt=Itō District|Q11498435|WD=Q11498435}} (Ibo Town Nakatomi, Tatsuno City) is the Engishiki Nakatomi Itate Shrine; that the Hachiman Shrine on Mount Matsuo in Okusami Village, Itō District (Hachiman, {{ill|lt=Hayashida Town|Q11533214|WD=Q11533214}}, {{ill|lt=Himeji City|Q209801|WD=Q209801}}) is the Engishiki Nakatomi Itate Shrine; and that the {{ill|lt=Uobuki Hachiman Shrine|Q11673954|WD=Q11673954}} located in Miyauchi Village, Itō District (Miyauchi, {{ill|lt=Aboshi Ward|Q11607184|WD=Q11607184}}, Himeji City) is the Engishiki Nakatomi Itate Shrine. === Engishiki Awaji Shrine === {{Shrine |Name = Awaji Shrine |Image = |Location = Enshrined together at Nakatomi Itate Shrine
Former location unknown |Coordinates = |Enshrined deities = {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Ōkashima-no-Kami}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkayama-toomi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} |Shrine rank = Traditionally Engishiki shrine |Founded = |Main hall = |Alternative name = |Pilgrimage site = |Annual festival = |Rituals = }} '''Awaji Shrine''' (あわちじんじゃ) is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki shrine|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}}. It is enshrined together at Nakatomi Itate Shrine in Ibo Town, Tatsuno City, Hyōgo Prefecture. During the Edo period, it was called Awaniwa Shrine. It is believed to have been enshrined together in the main hall of Nakatomi Itate Shrine sometime from the medieval period through the mid-Edo period, but the “Jingishi-ryō” identifies it with Yobira Shrine. === Access === * JR {{ill|lt=Kishin Line|Q869221|WD=Q869221}} {{ill|lt=Hon-Tatsuno Station|Q11520740|WD=Q11520740}}, about 10 minutes by taxi * JR {{ill|lt=San’yō Main Line|Q1193436|WD=Q1193436}}{{ill|lt=Tatsuno Station|Q5366259|WD=Q5366259}}, about 10 minutes by taxi * About 5 minutes north from the “Nishikama Intersection,” the terminus of {{ill|lt=National Route 2|Q1131340|WD=Q1131340}}{{ill|lt=Taishi–Tatsuno Bypass|Q11443881|WD=Q11443881}} === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Kumiai Chemical Industry|Q11298808|WD=Q11298808}} Tatsuno Plant * {{ill|lt=Hiraki (company)|Q11330294|WD=Q11330294|lt=Hiraki Co., Ltd.}} Tatsuno Store === Notes === === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Tsubuza Amaterasu Shrine|Q11604472|WD=Q11604472}} * {{ill|lt=Uobuki Hachiman Shrine|Q11673954|WD=Q11673954}} === External links === * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6318008.html Nakatomi Itate Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/dbindex/jinja/446001.html Nakatomi Itate Shrine (Ibo District, Harima Province)] (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program, “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection”) {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nakatomi Itate Shrine}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Hyōgo Prefecture}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nakatomi Itate Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nakatomi Itate Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:播磨国の式内社|名なかとみいたてしんしや]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧県社]] [[Category:揖保郡|社なかとみいたてしんしや]] [[Category:たつの市の建築物]] [[Category:たつの市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]]

haiden.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}{{ill|lt=Tatsuno City|Q877190|WD=Q877190}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ibo Town|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Ibo Town Nakatomi|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakatomi}} |Coordinates = {{WikiCoordinates2|34|50|27.3|N|134|32|27.4|E|scale:25000:region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
Also enshrines {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Ōkashima-no-Kami}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkayama-toomi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, the deities of the Engishiki-listed Awaji Shrine |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myōjin Taisha}})
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinsen-heihaku-ryō kyōshin-sha|1=UNKNOWN}} |Founded = Traditionally {{ill|lt=Hōki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676}} 1 ({{ill|lt=770|Q25021|WD=Q25021}}) |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |Alternative name = |Pilgrimage site = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} |Rituals = |Map= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Nakatomi
Itate Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Nakatomi Itate Shrine''' (なかとみ いたてじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290}}{{ill|lt=Tatsuno City|Q877190|WD=Q877190}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ibo Town|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Ibo Town Nakatomi|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakatomi}}. It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}}, classified as a {{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}, a former prefectural shrine, and a former shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and heihaku. Because it is located in the district named Nakatomi (pronounced Nakajin),The former {{ill|lt=Nishiharima|Q11628288|WD=Q11628288}} region’s {{ill|lt=Isei District|Q11498436|WD=Q11498436}} Nakatomi Village ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakajin Village|1=UNKNOWN}}), Nakatomi of {{ill|lt=Ibo Village|Q17193534|WD=Q17193534}}, later part of {{ill|lt=Tatsuno City|Q2570410|WD=Q2570410}} it is locally called “Nakajin–Indatsu Shrine,” using the on’yomi reading. It enshrines together the Engishiki-listed '''Awaji Shrine''' through {{ill|lt=enshrinement together|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} At present, it also enshrines {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Ōkashima-no-Kami}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkayama-toomi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, the deities of the Engishiki Awaji Shrine. === History === It is traditionally said to have been founded on the 15th day of the 6th month of {{ill|lt=Hōki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676}} 1, and it is listed as a {{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}. From the medieval period, however, due to {{ill|lt=Ryōbu Shintō|Q4402813|WD=Q4402813}}, ascetic practitioners became involved in shrine affairs and the Twelve Gongen that had been on the mountain behind the shrine were enshrined together in the main shrine. Until the early modern period, the shrine was called {{ill|lt=Zaō Gongen|Q10514059|WD=Q10514059}}. On October 10 of Meiji 10, after a formal petition, it reverted to its original name, Nakatomi Itate Shrine. In the same month and year,Also said to be May of Meiji 12 it was ranked as a prefectural shrine. === Chronology === : {{ill|lt=Hōki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676}} 1 ({{ill|lt=770|Q25021|WD=Q25021}}) June 15 – Foundation : {{ill|lt=Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021}}) – Five households granted as shrine estate to the Nakatomi deity : {{ill|lt=Shōtoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1713|Q6873|WD=Q6873}}) March – Construction : {{ill|lt=Man’en|Q1193862|WD=Q1193862}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1860|Q7691|WD=Q7691}}) – Construction : {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1877|Q7773|WD=Q7773}}) October 10 – Name restored to '''Nakatomi Itate Shrine''' : Meiji 12 ({{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783}}) May 15 – Ranked as a prefectural shrine : {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1916|Q2087|WD=Q2087}}) April 11 – Designated as a shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and heihaku === Shrine precincts === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki Tsubuza Amaterasu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Itsukushima Shrine|Q191763|WD=Q191763}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenman Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kitané Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yakushi Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q818468|lt=Benzaiten|1=UNKNOWN}} === The Tsubuoka legend and the Engishiki Tsubuza Amaterasu Shrine === The {{ill|lt=Harima no Kuni Fudoki|Q11498726|WD=Q11498726}} records that the name “Tsubuoka,” the origin of the name Ibo Village in {{ill|lt=Ibo District|Q1042150|WD=Q1042150}}, comes from the story that “when {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ashihara Shikō-no-Mikoto}} came to this mountain to dispute possession of the land with {{ill|lt=Amenohiboko|Q713100|WD=Q713100|lt=Ame-no-Hiboko-no-Mikoto}} and ate a meal, grains of rice fell.”Lines 283–288 This Tsubuoka is identified with Mount Nakatomi behind Nakatomi Itate Shrine, and the Engishiki Tsubuza Amaterasu Shrine stands within the precincts.Sources such as “Jingishi-ryō,” “Harima Kagami,” “Harima Meisho Junran Zue,” and “Ibo District Gazetteer” also suggest a connection between the Engishiki Myōjin Taisha Tsubuza Amaterasu Shrine and the Tsubuoka tradition at the Hachiman Shrine located in Iseyano, Hayashida Village, Ibo District (present-day Kamiiise, Hayashida-chō, Himeji City). === Nakatomi Itate Shrine as a candidate for an Engishiki shrine === There are several theories: that this shrine located in Nakajin Village of {{ill|lt=Itō District|Q11498435|WD=Q11498435}} (Ibo Town Nakatomi, Tatsuno City) is the Engishiki Nakatomi Itate Shrine; that the Hachiman Shrine on Mount Matsuo in Okusami Village, Itō District (Hachiman, {{ill|lt=Hayashida Town|Q11533214|WD=Q11533214}}, {{ill|lt=Himeji City|Q209801|WD=Q209801}}) is the Engishiki Nakatomi Itate Shrine; and that the {{ill|lt=Uobuki Hachiman Shrine|Q11673954|WD=Q11673954}} located in Miyauchi Village, Itō District (Miyauchi, {{ill|lt=Aboshi Ward|Q11607184|WD=Q11607184}}, Himeji City) is the Engishiki Nakatomi Itate Shrine. === Engishiki Awaji Shrine === {{Shrine |Name = Awaji Shrine |Image = |Location = Enshrined together at Nakatomi Itate Shrine
Former location unknown |Coordinates = |Enshrined deities = {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Ōkashima-no-Kami}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkayama-toomi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} |Shrine rank = Traditionally Engishiki shrine |Founded = |Main hall = |Alternative name = |Pilgrimage site = |Annual festival = |Rituals = }} '''Awaji Shrine''' (あわちじんじゃ) is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki shrine|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}}. It is enshrined together at Nakatomi Itate Shrine in Ibo Town, Tatsuno City, Hyōgo Prefecture. During the Edo period, it was called Awaniwa Shrine. It is believed to have been enshrined together in the main hall of Nakatomi Itate Shrine sometime from the medieval period through the mid-Edo period, but the “Jingishi-ryō” identifies it with Yobira Shrine. === Access === * JR {{ill|lt=Kishin Line|Q869221|WD=Q869221}} {{ill|lt=Hon-Tatsuno Station|Q11520740|WD=Q11520740}}, about 10 minutes by taxi * JR {{ill|lt=San’yō Main Line|Q1193436|WD=Q1193436}}{{ill|lt=Tatsuno Station|Q5366259|WD=Q5366259}}, about 10 minutes by taxi * About 5 minutes north from the “Nishikama Intersection,” the terminus of {{ill|lt=National Route 2|Q1131340|WD=Q1131340}}{{ill|lt=Taishi–Tatsuno Bypass|Q11443881|WD=Q11443881}} === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Kumiai Chemical Industry|Q11298808|WD=Q11298808}} Tatsuno Plant * {{ill|lt=Hiraki (company)|Q11330294|WD=Q11330294|lt=Hiraki Co., Ltd.}} Tatsuno Store === Notes === === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Tsubuza Amaterasu Shrine|Q11604472|WD=Q11604472}} * {{ill|lt=Uobuki Hachiman Shrine|Q11673954|WD=Q11673954}} === External links === * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6318008.html Nakatomi Itate Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/dbindex/jinja/446001.html Nakatomi Itate Shrine (Ibo District, Harima Province)] (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program, “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection”) {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nakatomi Itate Shrine}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Hyōgo Prefecture}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nakatomi Itate Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nakatomi Itate Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Harima Province]] [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:播磨国の式内社|名なかとみいたてしんしや]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧県社]] [[Category:揖保郡|社なかとみいたてしんしや]] [[Category:たつの市の建築物]] [[Category:たつの市の歴史]] [[Category:pages]]

= [[File:Nakatomi-Itate-jinja, haiden.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tatsuno City|Q877190|WD=Q877190|1=Tatsuno, Hyōgo}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ibo Town|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Ibo Town Nakatomi|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakatomi}} |Coordinates = {{WikiCoordinates2|34|50|27.3|N|134|32|27.4|E|scale:25000:region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}}
Also enshrines {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Ōkashima-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkayama-toomi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, the deities of the Engishiki-listed Awaji Shrine |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myōjin Taisha|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinsen-heihaku-ryō kyōshin-sha|1=UNKNOWN}} |Founded = Traditionally {{ill|lt=Hōki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676|1=Hōki}} 1 ({{ill|lt=770|Q25021|WD=Q25021|1=770}}) |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternative name = |Pilgrimage site = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} |Rituals = |Map= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Nakatomi
Itate Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Nakatomi Itate Shrine''' (なかとみ いたてじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tatsuno City|Q877190|WD=Q877190|1=Tatsuno, Hyōgo}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ibo Town|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Ibo Town Nakatomi|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakatomi}}. It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}}, classified as a {{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}, a former prefectural shrine, and a former shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and heihaku. Because it is located in the district named Nakatomi (pronounced Nakajin),The former {{ill|lt=Nishiharima|Q11628288|WD=Q11628288|1=Nishiharima}} region’s {{ill|lt=Isei District|Q11498436|WD=Q11498436|1=Issai district}} Nakatomi Village ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakajin Village|1=UNKNOWN}}), Nakatomi of {{ill|lt=Ibo Village|Q17193534|WD=Q17193534|1=Ibo}}, later part of {{ill|lt=Tatsuno City|Q2570410|WD=Q2570410|1=Tatsuno}} it is locally called “Nakajin–Indatsu Shrine,” using the on’yomi reading. It enshrines together the Engishiki-listed '''Awaji Shrine''' through {{ill|lt=enshrinement together|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524|1=Co-Enshrinement}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} At present, it also enshrines {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Ōkashima-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkayama-toomi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, the deities of the Engishiki Awaji Shrine. === History === It is traditionally said to have been founded on the 15th day of the 6th month of {{ill|lt=Hōki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676|1=Hōki}} 1, and it is listed as a {{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}. From the medieval period, however, due to {{ill|lt=Ryōbu Shintō|Q4402813|WD=Q4402813|1=Ryobu Shinto}}, ascetic practitioners became involved in shrine affairs and the Twelve Gongen that had been on the mountain behind the shrine were enshrined together in the main shrine. Until the early modern period, the shrine was called {{ill|lt=Zaō Gongen|Q10514059|WD=Q10514059|1=Zaō Gongen}}. On October 10 of Meiji 10, after a formal petition, it reverted to its original name, Nakatomi Itate Shrine. In the same month and year,Also said to be May of Meiji 12 it was ranked as a prefectural shrine. === Chronology === : {{ill|lt=Hōki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676|1=Hōki}} 1 ({{ill|lt=770|Q25021|WD=Q25021|1=770}}) June 15 – Foundation : {{ill|lt=Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}}) – Five households granted as shrine estate to the Nakatomi deity : {{ill|lt=Shōtoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1713|Q6873|WD=Q6873|1=1713}}) March – Construction : {{ill|lt=Man’en|Q1193862|WD=Q1193862|1=Man'en}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1860|Q7691|WD=Q7691|1=1860}}) – Construction : {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1877|Q7773|WD=Q7773|1=1877}}) October 10 – Name restored to '''Nakatomi Itate Shrine''' : Meiji 12 ({{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783|1=1879}}) May 15 – Ranked as a prefectural shrine : {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1916|Q2087|WD=Q2087|1=1916}}) April 11 – Designated as a shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and heihaku === Shrine precincts === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki Tsubuza Amaterasu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Itsukushima Shrine|Q191763|WD=Q191763|1=Itsukushima Shrine}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenman Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kitané Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yakushi Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q818468|lt=Benzaiten|1=Benzaiten}} === The Tsubuoka legend and the Engishiki Tsubuza Amaterasu Shrine === The {{ill|lt=Harima no Kuni Fudoki|Q11498726|WD=Q11498726|1=Harima no Kuni Fudoki}} records that the name “Tsubuoka,” the origin of the name Ibo Village in {{ill|lt=Ibo District|Q1042150|WD=Q1042150|1=Ibo District, Hyōgo}}, comes from the story that “when {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ashihara Shikō-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} came to this mountain to dispute possession of the land with {{ill|lt=Amenohiboko|Q713100|WD=Q713100|lt=Ame-no-Hiboko-no-Mikoto|1=Amenohiboko}} and ate a meal, grains of rice fell.”Lines 283–288 This Tsubuoka is identified with Mount Nakatomi behind Nakatomi Itate Shrine, and the Engishiki Tsubuza Amaterasu Shrine stands within the precincts.Sources such as “Jingishi-ryō,” “Harima Kagami,” “Harima Meisho Junran Zue,” and “Ibo District Gazetteer” also suggest a connection between the Engishiki Myōjin Taisha Tsubuza Amaterasu Shrine and the Tsubuoka tradition at the Hachiman Shrine located in Iseyano, Hayashida Village, Ibo District (present-day Kamiiise, Hayashida-chō, Himeji City). === Nakatomi Itate Shrine as a candidate for an Engishiki shrine === There are several theories: that this shrine located in Nakajin Village of {{ill|lt=Itō District|Q11498435|WD=Q11498435|1=Ittō district}} (Ibo Town Nakatomi, Tatsuno City) is the Engishiki Nakatomi Itate Shrine; that the Hachiman Shrine on Mount Matsuo in Okusami Village, Itō District (Hachiman, {{ill|lt=Hayashida Town|Q11533214|WD=Q11533214|1=Hayashida}}, {{ill|lt=Himeji City|Q209801|WD=Q209801|1=Himeji}}) is the Engishiki Nakatomi Itate Shrine; and that the {{ill|lt=Uobuki Hachiman Shrine|Q11673954|WD=Q11673954|1=Usuki Hachiman Shrine}} located in Miyauchi Village, Itō District (Miyauchi, {{ill|lt=Aboshi Ward|Q11607184|WD=Q11607184|1=Aboshi}}, Himeji City) is the Engishiki Nakatomi Itate Shrine. === Engishiki Awaji Shrine === {{Shrine |Name = Awaji Shrine |Image = |Location = Enshrined together at Nakatomi Itate Shrine
Former location unknown |Coordinates = |Enshrined deities = {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Ōkashima-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkayama-toomi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} |Shrine rank = Traditionally Engishiki shrine |Founded = |Main hall = |Alternative name = |Pilgrimage site = |Annual festival = |Rituals = }} '''Awaji Shrine''' (あわちじんじゃ) is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki shrine|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}. It is enshrined together at Nakatomi Itate Shrine in Ibo Town, Tatsuno City, Hyōgo Prefecture. During the Edo period, it was called Awaniwa Shrine. It is believed to have been enshrined together in the main hall of Nakatomi Itate Shrine sometime from the medieval period through the mid-Edo period, but the “Jingishi-ryō” identifies it with Yobira Shrine. === Access === * JR {{ill|lt=Kishin Line|Q869221|WD=Q869221|1=Kishin Line}} {{ill|lt=Hon-Tatsuno Station|Q11520740|WD=Q11520740|1=Hon-Tatsuno Station}}, about 10 minutes by taxi * JR {{ill|lt=San’yō Main Line|Q1193436|WD=Q1193436|1=San'yō Main Line}}{{ill|lt=Tatsuno Station|Q5366259|WD=Q5366259|1=Tatsuno Station (Hyōgo)}}, about 10 minutes by taxi * About 5 minutes north from the “Nishikama Intersection,” the terminus of {{ill|lt=National Route 2|Q1131340|WD=Q1131340|1=Japan National Route 2}}{{ill|lt=Taishi–Tatsuno Bypass|Q11443881|WD=Q11443881|1=Prince Tatsuno bypass}} === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Kumiai Chemical Industry|Q11298808|WD=Q11298808|1=Kumiai Kagaku Kōgyō}} Tatsuno Plant * {{ill|lt=Hiraki (company)|Q11330294|WD=Q11330294|lt=Hiraki Co., Ltd.|1=Hiraki}} Tatsuno Store === Notes === === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Tsubuza Amaterasu Shrine|Q11604472|WD=Q11604472|1=Iibo Nimasu Amaterasu Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Uobuki Hachiman Shrine|Q11673954|WD=Q11673954|1=Usuki Hachiman Shrine}} === External links === * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6318008.html Nakatomi Itate Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/dbindex/jinja/446001.html Nakatomi Itate Shrine (Ibo District, Harima Province)] (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program, “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection”) {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nakatomi Itate Shrine}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Hyōgo Prefecture}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha Worship hall |Location = {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tatsuno City|Q877190|WD=Q877190|1=Tatsuno, Hyōgo}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ibo Town|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Ibo Town Nakatomi|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakatomi}} |Coordinates = {{WikiCoordinates2|34|50|27.3|N|134|32|27.4|E|scale:25000:region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}}
Also enshrines {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Ōkashima-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkayama-toomi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, the deities of the Engishiki-listed Awaji Shrine |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myōjin Taisha|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinsen-heihaku-ryō kyōshin-sha|1=UNKNOWN}} |Founded = Traditionally {{ill|lt=Hōki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676|1=Hōki}} 1 ({{ill|lt=770|Q25021|WD=Q25021|1=770}}) |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternative name = |Pilgrimage site = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} |Rituals = |Map= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Nakatomi
Itate Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Nakatomi Itate Shrine''' (なかとみ いたてじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tatsuno City|Q877190|WD=Q877190|1=Tatsuno, Hyōgo}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ibo Town|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Ibo Town Nakatomi|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakatomi}}. It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}}, classified as a {{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}, a former prefectural shrine, and a former shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and heihaku. Because it is located in the district named Nakatomi (pronounced Nakajin),The former {{ill|lt=Nishiharima|Q11628288|WD=Q11628288|1=Nishiharima}} region’s {{ill|lt=Isei District|Q11498436|WD=Q11498436|1=Issai district}} Nakatomi Village ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakajin Village|1=UNKNOWN}}), Nakatomi of {{ill|lt=Ibo Village|Q17193534|WD=Q17193534|1=Ibo}}, later part of {{ill|lt=Tatsuno City|Q2570410|WD=Q2570410|1=Tatsuno}} it is locally called “Nakajin–Indatsu Shrine,” using the on’yomi reading. It enshrines together the Engishiki-listed '''Awaji Shrine''' through {{ill|lt=enshrinement together|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524|1=Co-Enshrinement}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} At present, it also enshrines {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Ōkashima-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkayama-toomi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, the deities of the Engishiki Awaji Shrine. === History === It is traditionally said to have been founded on the 15th day of the 6th month of {{ill|lt=Hōki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676|1=Hōki}} 1, and it is listed as a {{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}. From the medieval period, however, due to {{ill|lt=Ryōbu Shintō|Q4402813|WD=Q4402813|1=Ryobu Shinto}}, ascetic practitioners became involved in shrine affairs and the Twelve Gongen that had been on the mountain behind the shrine were enshrined together in the main shrine. Until the early modern period, the shrine was called {{ill|lt=Zaō Gongen|Q10514059|WD=Q10514059|1=Zaō Gongen}}. On October 10 of Meiji 10, after a formal petition, it reverted to its original name, Nakatomi Itate Shrine. In the same month and year,Also said to be May of Meiji 12 it was ranked as a prefectural shrine. === Chronology === : {{ill|lt=Hōki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676|1=Hōki}} 1 ({{ill|lt=770|Q25021|WD=Q25021|1=770}}) June 15 – Foundation : {{ill|lt=Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}}) – Five households granted as shrine estate to the Nakatomi deity : {{ill|lt=Shōtoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1713|Q6873|WD=Q6873|1=1713}}) March – Construction : {{ill|lt=Man’en|Q1193862|WD=Q1193862|1=Man'en}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1860|Q7691|WD=Q7691|1=1860}}) – Construction : {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1877|Q7773|WD=Q7773|1=1877}}) October 10 – Name restored to '''Nakatomi Itate Shrine''' : Meiji 12 ({{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783|1=1879}}) May 15 – Ranked as a prefectural shrine : {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1916|Q2087|WD=Q2087|1=1916}}) April 11 – Designated as a shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and heihaku === Shrine precincts === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki Tsubuza Amaterasu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Itsukushima Shrine|Q191763|WD=Q191763|1=Itsukushima Shrine}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenman Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kitané Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yakushi Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q818468|lt=Benzaiten|1=Benzaiten}} === The Tsubuoka legend and the Engishiki Tsubuza Amaterasu Shrine === The {{ill|lt=Harima no Kuni Fudoki|Q11498726|WD=Q11498726|1=Harima no Kuni Fudoki}} records that the name “Tsubuoka,” the origin of the name Ibo Village in {{ill|lt=Ibo District|Q1042150|WD=Q1042150|1=Ibo District, Hyōgo}}, comes from the story that “when {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ashihara Shikō-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} came to this mountain to dispute possession of the land with {{ill|lt=Amenohiboko|Q713100|WD=Q713100|lt=Ame-no-Hiboko-no-Mikoto|1=Amenohiboko}} and ate a meal, grains of rice fell.”Lines 283–288 This Tsubuoka is identified with Mount Nakatomi behind Nakatomi Itate Shrine, and the Engishiki Tsubuza Amaterasu Shrine stands within the precincts.Sources such as “Jingishi-ryō,” “Harima Kagami,” “Harima Meisho Junran Zue,” and “Ibo District Gazetteer” also suggest a connection between the Engishiki Myōjin Taisha Tsubuza Amaterasu Shrine and the Tsubuoka tradition at the Hachiman Shrine located in Iseyano, Hayashida Village, Ibo District (present-day Kamiiise, Hayashida-chō, Himeji City). === Nakatomi Itate Shrine as a candidate for an Engishiki shrine === There are several theories: that this shrine located in Nakajin Village of {{ill|lt=Itō District|Q11498435|WD=Q11498435|1=Ittō district}} (Ibo Town Nakatomi, Tatsuno City) is the Engishiki Nakatomi Itate Shrine; that the Hachiman Shrine on Mount Matsuo in Okusami Village, Itō District (Hachiman, {{ill|lt=Hayashida Town|Q11533214|WD=Q11533214|1=Hayashida}}, {{ill|lt=Himeji City|Q209801|WD=Q209801|1=Himeji}}) is the Engishiki Nakatomi Itate Shrine; and that the {{ill|lt=Uobuki Hachiman Shrine|Q11673954|WD=Q11673954|1=Usuki Hachiman Shrine}} located in Miyauchi Village, Itō District (Miyauchi, {{ill|lt=Aboshi Ward|Q11607184|WD=Q11607184|1=Aboshi}}, Himeji City) is the Engishiki Nakatomi Itate Shrine. === Engishiki Awaji Shrine === {{Shrine |Name = Awaji Shrine |Image = |Location = Enshrined together at Nakatomi Itate Shrine
Former location unknown |Coordinates = |Enshrined deities = {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Ōkashima-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkayama-toomi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} |Shrine rank = Traditionally Engishiki shrine |Founded = |Main hall = |Alternative name = |Pilgrimage site = |Annual festival = |Rituals = }} '''Awaji Shrine''' (あわちじんじゃ) is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki shrine|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}. It is enshrined together at Nakatomi Itate Shrine in Ibo Town, Tatsuno City, Hyōgo Prefecture. During the Edo period, it was called Awaniwa Shrine. It is believed to have been enshrined together in the main hall of Nakatomi Itate Shrine sometime from the medieval period through the mid-Edo period, but the “Jingishi-ryō” identifies it with Yobira Shrine. === Access === * JR {{ill|lt=Kishin Line|Q869221|WD=Q869221|1=Kishin Line}} {{ill|lt=Hon-Tatsuno Station|Q11520740|WD=Q11520740|1=Hon-Tatsuno Station}}, about 10 minutes by taxi * JR {{ill|lt=San’yō Main Line|Q1193436|WD=Q1193436|1=San'yō Main Line}}{{ill|lt=Tatsuno Station|Q5366259|WD=Q5366259|1=Tatsuno Station (Hyōgo)}}, about 10 minutes by taxi * About 5 minutes north from the “Nishikama Intersection,” the terminus of {{ill|lt=National Route 2|Q1131340|WD=Q1131340|1=Japan National Route 2}}{{ill|lt=Taishi–Tatsuno Bypass|Q11443881|WD=Q11443881|1=Prince Tatsuno bypass}} === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Kumiai Chemical Industry|Q11298808|WD=Q11298808|1=Kumiai Kagaku Kōgyō}} Tatsuno Plant * {{ill|lt=Hiraki (company)|Q11330294|WD=Q11330294|lt=Hiraki Co., Ltd.|1=Hiraki}} Tatsuno Store === Notes === === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Tsubuza Amaterasu Shrine|Q11604472|WD=Q11604472|1=Iibo Nimasu Amaterasu Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Uobuki Hachiman Shrine|Q11673954|WD=Q11673954|1=Usuki Hachiman Shrine}} === External links === * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6318008.html Nakatomi Itate Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/dbindex/jinja/446001.html Nakatomi Itate Shrine (Ibo District, Harima Province)] (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program, “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection”) {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nakatomi Itate Shrine}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Hyōgo Prefecture}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nakatomi Itate Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nakatomi Itate Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] Also enshrines {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Ōkashima-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkayama-toomi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, the deities of the Engishiki-listed Awaji Shrine |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myōjin Taisha|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinsen-heihaku-ryō kyōshin-sha|1=UNKNOWN}} |Founded = Traditionally {{ill|lt=Hōki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676|1=Hōki}} 1 ({{ill|lt=770|Q25021|WD=Q25021|1=770}}) |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternative name = |Pilgrimage site = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} |Rituals = |Map= {{Location map|Japan Hyogo|label=Nakatomi
Itate Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Nakatomi Itate Shrine''' (なかとみ いたてじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|WD=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Tatsuno City|Q877190|WD=Q877190|1=Tatsuno, Hyōgo}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ibo Town|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Ibo Town Nakatomi|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakatomi}}. It is an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki shrine|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}}, classified as a {{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}, a former prefectural shrine, and a former shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and heihaku. Because it is located in the district named Nakatomi (pronounced Nakajin),The former {{ill|lt=Nishiharima|Q11628288|WD=Q11628288|1=Nishiharima}} region’s {{ill|lt=Isei District|Q11498436|WD=Q11498436|1=Issai district}} Nakatomi Village ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakajin Village|1=UNKNOWN}}), Nakatomi of {{ill|lt=Ibo Village|Q17193534|WD=Q17193534|1=Ibo}}, later part of {{ill|lt=Tatsuno City|Q2570410|WD=Q2570410|1=Tatsuno}} it is locally called “Nakajin–Indatsu Shrine,” using the on’yomi reading. It enshrines together the Engishiki-listed '''Awaji Shrine''' through {{ill|lt=enshrinement together|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524|1=Co-Enshrinement}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} At present, it also enshrines {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Ōkashima-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkayama-toomi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, the deities of the Engishiki Awaji Shrine. === History === It is traditionally said to have been founded on the 15th day of the 6th month of {{ill|lt=Hōki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676|1=Hōki}} 1, and it is listed as a {{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}. From the medieval period, however, due to {{ill|lt=Ryōbu Shintō|Q4402813|WD=Q4402813|1=Ryobu Shinto}}, ascetic practitioners became involved in shrine affairs and the Twelve Gongen that had been on the mountain behind the shrine were enshrined together in the main shrine. Until the early modern period, the shrine was called {{ill|lt=Zaō Gongen|Q10514059|WD=Q10514059|1=Zaō Gongen}}. On October 10 of Meiji 10, after a formal petition, it reverted to its original name, Nakatomi Itate Shrine. In the same month and year,Also said to be May of Meiji 12 it was ranked as a prefectural shrine. === Chronology === : {{ill|lt=Hōki|Q18520676|WD=Q18520676|1=Hōki}} 1 ({{ill|lt=770|Q25021|WD=Q25021|1=770}}) June 15 – Foundation : {{ill|lt=Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} 1 ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}}) – Five households granted as shrine estate to the Nakatomi deity : {{ill|lt=Shōtoku (Japan)|Q1193848|WD=Q1193848|lt=Shōtoku|1=Shōtoku (era)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1713|Q6873|WD=Q6873|1=1713}}) March – Construction : {{ill|lt=Man’en|Q1193862|WD=Q1193862|1=Man'en}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1860|Q7691|WD=Q7691|1=1860}}) – Construction : {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 10 ({{ill|lt=1877|Q7773|WD=Q7773|1=1877}}) October 10 – Name restored to '''Nakatomi Itate Shrine''' : Meiji 12 ({{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|WD=Q7783|1=1879}}) May 15 – Ranked as a prefectural shrine : {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1916|Q2087|WD=Q2087|1=1916}}) April 11 – Designated as a shrine receiving offerings of sacred food and heihaku === Shrine precincts === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki Tsubuza Amaterasu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Itsukushima Shrine|Q191763|WD=Q191763|1=Itsukushima Shrine}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenman Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kitané Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yakushi Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q818468|lt=Benzaiten|1=Benzaiten}} === The Tsubuoka legend and the Engishiki Tsubuza Amaterasu Shrine === The {{ill|lt=Harima no Kuni Fudoki|Q11498726|WD=Q11498726|1=Harima no Kuni Fudoki}} records that the name “Tsubuoka,” the origin of the name Ibo Village in {{ill|lt=Ibo District|Q1042150|WD=Q1042150|1=Ibo District, Hyōgo}}, comes from the story that “when {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ashihara Shikō-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} came to this mountain to dispute possession of the land with {{ill|lt=Amenohiboko|Q713100|WD=Q713100|lt=Ame-no-Hiboko-no-Mikoto|1=Amenohiboko}} and ate a meal, grains of rice fell.”Lines 283–288 This Tsubuoka is identified with Mount Nakatomi behind Nakatomi Itate Shrine, and the Engishiki Tsubuza Amaterasu Shrine stands within the precincts.Sources such as “Jingishi-ryō,” “Harima Kagami,” “Harima Meisho Junran Zue,” and “Ibo District Gazetteer” also suggest a connection between the Engishiki Myōjin Taisha Tsubuza Amaterasu Shrine and the Tsubuoka tradition at the Hachiman Shrine located in Iseyano, Hayashida Village, Ibo District (present-day Kamiiise, Hayashida-chō, Himeji City). === Nakatomi Itate Shrine as a candidate for an Engishiki shrine === There are several theories: that this shrine located in Nakajin Village of {{ill|lt=Itō District|Q11498435|WD=Q11498435|1=Ittō district}} (Ibo Town Nakatomi, Tatsuno City) is the Engishiki Nakatomi Itate Shrine; that the Hachiman Shrine on Mount Matsuo in Okusami Village, Itō District (Hachiman, {{ill|lt=Hayashida Town|Q11533214|WD=Q11533214|1=Hayashida}}, {{ill|lt=Himeji City|Q209801|WD=Q209801|1=Himeji}}) is the Engishiki Nakatomi Itate Shrine; and that the {{ill|lt=Uobuki Hachiman Shrine|Q11673954|WD=Q11673954|1=Usuki Hachiman Shrine}} located in Miyauchi Village, Itō District (Miyauchi, {{ill|lt=Aboshi Ward|Q11607184|WD=Q11607184|1=Aboshi}}, Himeji City) is the Engishiki Nakatomi Itate Shrine. === Engishiki Awaji Shrine === {{Shrine |Name = Awaji Shrine |Image = |Location = Enshrined together at Nakatomi Itate Shrine
Former location unknown |Coordinates = |Enshrined deities = {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Ōkashima-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōkayama-toomi-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}} |Shrine rank = Traditionally Engishiki shrine |Founded = |Main hall = |Alternative name = |Pilgrimage site = |Annual festival = |Rituals = }} '''Awaji Shrine''' (あわちじんじゃ) is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki shrine|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}. It is enshrined together at Nakatomi Itate Shrine in Ibo Town, Tatsuno City, Hyōgo Prefecture. During the Edo period, it was called Awaniwa Shrine. It is believed to have been enshrined together in the main hall of Nakatomi Itate Shrine sometime from the medieval period through the mid-Edo period, but the “Jingishi-ryō” identifies it with Yobira Shrine. === Access === * JR {{ill|lt=Kishin Line|Q869221|WD=Q869221|1=Kishin Line}} {{ill|lt=Hon-Tatsuno Station|Q11520740|WD=Q11520740|1=Hon-Tatsuno Station}}, about 10 minutes by taxi * JR {{ill|lt=San’yō Main Line|Q1193436|WD=Q1193436|1=San'yō Main Line}}{{ill|lt=Tatsuno Station|Q5366259|WD=Q5366259|1=Tatsuno Station (Hyōgo)}}, about 10 minutes by taxi * About 5 minutes north from the “Nishikama Intersection,” the terminus of {{ill|lt=National Route 2|Q1131340|WD=Q1131340|1=Japan National Route 2}}{{ill|lt=Taishi–Tatsuno Bypass|Q11443881|WD=Q11443881|1=Prince Tatsuno bypass}} === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Kumiai Chemical Industry|Q11298808|WD=Q11298808|1=Kumiai Kagaku Kōgyō}} Tatsuno Plant * {{ill|lt=Hiraki (company)|Q11330294|WD=Q11330294|lt=Hiraki Co., Ltd.|1=Hiraki}} Tatsuno Store === Notes === === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Tsubuza Amaterasu Shrine|Q11604472|WD=Q11604472|1=Iibo Nimasu Amaterasu Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Uobuki Hachiman Shrine|Q11673954|WD=Q11673954|1=Usuki Hachiman Shrine}} === External links === * [http://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6318008.html Nakatomi Itate Shrine] (Hyōgo Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/dbindex/jinja/446001.html Nakatomi Itate Shrine (Ibo District, Harima Province)] (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program, “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection”) {{Shinto-stub}} {{shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Hyōgo Prefecture}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Nakatomi Itate Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Nakatomi Itate Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]
